ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic chocolate beverage class; no specific entry for Iced Swiss Chocolate as a Singapore café-style cold drink. Closest defensible parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers chocolate-flavoured drinks broadly; no Singapore-specific iced variant code exists. Closest defensible parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for iced chocolate beverage in the clinical terminology. Not required for this food type.

serving.natural_language_portion: One regular cup of Iced Swiss Chocolate is approximately 350 g, equivalent to a standard café serving size commonly sold in Singapore food courts and cafés.

nutrient_source.title: Iced Swiss Chocolate — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database (core macros and primary minerals); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy Food #14079 Beverages, chocolate-flavour mix, no sugar added, prepared with water (saturated fat, trans fat, vitamin C adaptation)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore; U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 42 kcal, protein 0.0 g, fat 0.25 g, carbohydrate 10.36 g, sugar 9.94 g, sodium 3 mg, dietary fibre 0.0 g, potassium 5 mg, calcium 1 mg, magnesium 0 mg, iron 0.02 mg, cholesterol 0 mg) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C were not available from HPB Foodo for this record and were adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (chocolate-flavour beverage mix prepared with water, FDC ID 174052) as the closest approved-source equivalent. The original input values for saturated fat (2.0 g/100g from GrabFood) and trans fat (0.09 g/100g from GrabFood) were rejected as GrabFood is a forbidden source and the saturated fat value was physically implausible given total fat of 0.25 g/100g. USDA-derived saturated fat 0.15 g/100g, trans fat 0.0 g/100g, and vitamin C 0.0 mg/100g are used in their place. Adaptation is documented and plausible given the similar dilute chocolate-syrup-in-water/milk preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of chocolate-flavoured milk drinks and sugar-sweetened chocolate beverages — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Iced Swiss Chocolate as a Singapore café product in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for chocolate-flavoured milk drinks and sugar-sweetened chocolate beverages, which range from approximately 34 (low-fat chocolate milk) to 68 (chocolate-flavoured sugar-sweetened drinks). Given the high sugar content (9.94 g/100g) and zero dietary fibre, a GI of 60 is a conservative mid-range analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 36.26) / 100 = 21.8, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 10.36 × 3.5 = 36.26 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk via the AHA-identified sugar-driven sodium-retention pathway, despite low sodium (3 mg/100g).
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage; if consumed, limit to occasional small servings and request an unsweetened cocoa-based alternative with plain low-fat milk to reduce the sugar-driven blood pressure elevation risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94 g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre causes rapid postprandial glucose absorption; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 34.8 g of sugar with no fibre buffer.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage as it is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage under ADA guidelines; substitute with unsweetened cocoa powder in low-fat milk to eliminate added sugar and blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar (9.94 g/100g) contributes to atherogenic dyslipidaemia and cardiovascular risk; saturated fat (0.15 g/100g) and trans fat (0.0 g/100g) are low and not the primary concern.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage due to its high added sugar content, which independently raises cardiovascular risk; opt for an unsweetened cocoa-based drink to reduce sugar-driven atherogenic risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined sugar (9.94 g/100g) with zero dietary fibre and no anti-inflammatory micronutrients promotes pro-inflammatory mediators relevant to RA disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage and substitute with an unsweetened cocoa drink using plant-based or low-fat milk to reduce refined sugar intake and limit pro-inflammatory stimulus.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Swiss Chocolate is a cold chocolate-based beverage popular in Singapore cafés, food courts, and casual dining establishments. It is typically prepared by blending or mixing Swiss-style chocolate syrup or powder with chilled milk and serving over ice, sometimes topped with whipped cream. The drink has a sweet, rich chocolate flavour with a smooth, cold texture. Per 100 g, it provides 42 kcal, 10.36 g carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), 0.25 g fat (saturated fat 0.15 g, trans fat 0.0 g), and negligible protein (0.0 g confirmed per HPB Foodo). Dietary fibre is absent (0.0 g), making this a low-nutrient-density sugar-sweetened beverage. A standard 350 g serving delivers approximately 147 kcal and 34.8 g of sugar. Clinically, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under ADA and AHA guidelines due to its high sugar content and zero fibre, posing elevated risk for postprandial glucose spikes in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and contributing to blood pressure elevation via sugar-driven pathways in hypertensive patients. The high added sugar content is also the primary cardiovascular concern, as saturated and trans fat levels are low. The beverage is vegetarian, halal-suitable, and gluten-free. It is commonly consumed as a dessert drink or afternoon refreshment in Singapore's multicultural food culture, enjoyed across Malay, Chinese, Indian, and other communities.

Is Iced Swiss Chocolate suitable for diabetics?

How much sugar is in a cup of Iced Swiss Chocolate?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174052) as the closest approved-source equivalent after the original GrabFood-sourced values were rejected as a forbidden source and physically implausible. This adaptation reduces overall confidence to medium. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries with documented calculation basis. Multilingual names provided for all mandatory Singaporean cuisine languages.
notes: 1. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo — this is a dilute chocolate-syrup-and-milk beverage where protein contribution falls below the HPB reporting threshold (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database). dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo — no fibre-containing ingredients are present in the standard preparation. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. magnesium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174052). trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174052). 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (-1): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1 — detailed fatty acid breakdown for this beverage is not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific product as of 2026-04. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1 — these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this beverage and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific Singapore product as of 2026-04. 3. SOURCE CORRECTION: The original input values for saturated_fat (2.0 g/100g), trans_fat (0.09 g/100g), and vitamin_c (0.0 mg/100g) were attributed to GrabFood, a forbidden source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. The saturated fat value of 2.0 g/100g was also physically implausible as it exceeded total fat (0.25 g/100g). These three fields were re-sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174052, chocolate-flavour beverage mix prepared with water) as the closest approved-source equivalent. 4. CULTURAL NOTE: Although the input record classifies this as Malay cuisine, Iced Swiss Chocolate is a pan-Singaporean café beverage not specific to Malay cuisine; it is widely consumed across all ethnic groups in Singapore. The cuisine classification has been updated to Singaporean. 5. GI ESTIMATION: GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database chocolate-flavoured beverage entries; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the approved GI database.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Iced Swiss Chocolate', a precise canonical English name for this Singapore café beverage. All mandatory Singaporean multilingual names present: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (42.0), protein_g (0.0, confirmed zero per HPB Foodo), fat_g (0.25), carbohydrate_g (10.36) all present and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat (0.15 g), trans fat (0.0 g), vitamin C (0.0 mg) adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174052 — an approved primary source. GrabFood forbidden source fully removed. nutrient_source organisation and URL reference only HPB and USDA, both approved sources.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (3.0), saturated_fat_g (0.15), sugar_g (9.94) all present as numeric values sourced from approved sources (HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central).

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (60), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (21.8), gi_source all present. GI source organisation is University of Sydney GI Database — an approved GI source. Analogous estimate basis fully documented with calculation shown.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn closest_parent match to chocolate beverage class (FOODON_03301710) documented with gap note. FoodEx2 A0CKP (chocolate-flavoured drink) applied as closest_parent match with gap note. SNOMED CT null with documented gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above 200 characters. Covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension and cardiovascular disease, and key nutrient profile.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate), Type 2 Diabetes (high, human_condition_name correctly set to 'Type 2 Diabetes', machine key type2_diabetes), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Additional condition rheumatoid_arthritis included from input data.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases flat array contains 11 entries covering English variants, Malay, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Tamil, Hindi, and abbreviations. aliases_by_language populated for all mandatory Singaporean languages. Self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array — passed.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built from input JSON with HPB Foodo as primary source.

Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'. Attempted re-sourcing of saturated fat, trans fat, vitamin C from USDA FoodData Central but nutrient_source URL still referenced a non-approved path.

Iteration 3: Fully replaced all GrabFood-sourced nutrient values (saturated_fat, trans_fat, vitamin_c) with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174052 values. Updated nutrient_source title, organisation, and URL to reference only HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central — both approved sources. Removed all references to GrabFood from nutrient_source. Updated notes to document source correction. Updated confidence_reason to reflect adapted sourcing. GI source confirmed as University of Sydney GI Database throughout.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Iced Swiss Chocolate (G00011040) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard after three iterations. The critical fix in iteration 3 was the complete removal of GrabFood as a nutrient source and full replacement of the three affected fields (saturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg) with values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174052), an approved source. Core macros remain sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo at confidence 0.95. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries with full documentation. All mandatory Singaporean multilingual names are present. Aliases are comprehensive and internally consistent. Zero-value macros and -1 sentinel values are fully justified and documented.
